Proposal Sustainability & cost model

The greener choice is also the cheaper one.

Scenario
The council estate

How many buildings of each size take part. Larger buildings cost and save proportionally more.

Buildings in total 106
Location and grid

Each option sets the local grid’s carbon (kg CO₂ per kWh) and the electricity price ($ per kWh).

Upgrades in the package

Select the upgrades to include. The estimate beside each is how long it takes to pay back.

Net saving, per year
$2.01M
Once the upgrades have paid for themselves, this stays in the budget every year.
Pays for itself in
Year 6
From then on it saves money every year it keeps running.
Emissions cut, per year
5,168t CO₂
Carbon taken off the books each year at your chosen grid.
Electricity saved, per year
7.60GWh
Power the buildings no longer need to draw from the grid.

Conservative by design. Every dollar figure above holds electricity at the Australian average price of $0.28 per kWh for all fifteen years, with no allowance for inflation. Australian tariffs have risen over the long run, so this is close to a worst case: as power gets dearer the yearly saving grows and the payback arrives sooner, and over fifteen years even modest annual rises compound into a materially larger return.

The council estate
Every building in the scenario, to scale. Large buildings sit at the civic core, small ones at the edge. A green roof marks a building carrying the upgrade package.

Cash and carbon over the first 15 years
Year 1 is the outlay. From Year 6 the council is ahead, and the surplus grows every year after. The carbon and electricity it saves keep building the whole time.
Most spent up front
$10.0M
The full outlay, recovered within 6 years.
Position by year 15

What the programme has returned by then.
Cars off the road
1,123
The same yearly carbon as taking this many petrol cars off the road.

1,267
The electricity saved each year would run about this many average homes.

246,095

E-waste diverted
42,120 kg
Old lighting and IT kept out of landfill when the work is done.
Which upgrades to do first
Each upgrade shown by what it costs and how long it takes to pay back, for a typical (medium) building. The larger the circle, the more carbon it saves each year. Start at the bottom left and work up.

Where it is built changes what it saves
The electricity saved is the same everywhere. A coal-heavy grid avoids far more carbon for each unit than a hydro or solar one. Figures are per typical (medium) building, per year; your chosen grid is highlighted.
Current technology vs the greener alternative
What each upgrade replaces, and what the council saves per typical (medium) building each year by making the greener choice. Upgrades not in the current package are dimmed.
One rollout keeps 42,120 kg out of landfill
Replacing old lighting and IT responsibly is a one-off gain when the work is done, on top of the yearly savings. Each type of equipment has a clear destination.

Ask the dashboard Retrieval only

Every answer is grounded in this dashboard's live figures, which are re-read each time you ask. Without a key it answers straight from those figures, with no language model involved.
